checkpoint_state: post-lambda / post-sigma-b / post-safe-phi-b / post-upsilon-a / post-phi-b3 / post-upsilon-b / post-upsilon-c / pre-phi-b4-or-other-next-block

hotswap_boundary_reconsideration.summary: Completed Φ-B3 plus Υ-B0..Υ-B2 plus Υ-C0..Υ-C2 now provide enough upstream law to define hotswap boundaries honestly, but not to claim live cutover safety.

distributed_authority_reconsideration.summary: Distributed authority still lacks authority handoff, deterministic replication posture, quorum proof, and live trust convergence maturity.

further_release_control_operational_matu.summary: Additional Υ work remains possible, but the best next blocker reducer is now Φ-B4 because the remaining unresolved gap is runtime cutover boundary law.

zeta_blocker_reduction.summary: The completed band closes most release-control ambiguity, but Ζ still remains blocked by hotswap, distributed authority, live trust rotation, and live-cutover continuity gaps.

ambiguities_eliminated: completed phi-b3 plus upsilon-b and upsilon-c does not automatically make phi-b5 reconsiderable
more release doctrine does not equal live runtime cutover proof
further upsilon work is no longer automatically the best move before phi-b4

risky_phi_b_readiness.title: HOTSWAP_BOUNDARIES-0
risky_phi_b_readiness.summary: Hotswap boundaries are now ready to be defined because coexistence, lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, rehearsal, rollback, canary, downgrade, and trust continuity layers are explicit, but live cutover safety remains unproven.

risky_phi_b_readiness.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS-0
risky_phi_b_readiness.summary: Distributed authority remains premature because shard relocation is still unrealistic currently, while authority handoff, replication proof, and distributed trust convergence remain unresolved.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_TRUST_ROTATION_AND_REVOCATION_PROPAGATION_PREREQUISITES-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Trust-root state remains empty, trust policy remains provisional, and live propagation should stay downstream of Φ-B4.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_CUTOVER_RECEIPTS_AND_RUNTIME_CONTINUITY-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Receipt and provenance law is explicit, but live-cutover generalization depends on the runtime boundary law that Φ-B4 must define first.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: PUBLICATION_AND_TRUST_OPERATIONALIZATION_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Publication and trust doctrine are explicit, but live trust and publication execution remain too immature to operationalize before Φ-B4.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_RELEASE_CONTROLLER_AND_AUTOMATION_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Further automation work is plausible later, but it is no longer the main blocker reducer before Φ-B4.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.summary: Runtime cutover, replacement, and partial live exchange still need explicit lawful boundary grammar.
Authority handoff, deterministic replication posture, quorum semantics, and shard relocation proof remain unresolved.
Trust policy remains provisional and trust-root state remains empty, keeping live rotation blocked.
Revocation continuity is explicit, but live propagation and runtime-sensitive trust transition choreography remain unproven.
Receipts and provenance continuity are explicit, but they are not yet generalized across live runtime replacement or trust cutover.
The runtime doctrine stack is explicit, but lawful cutover proof is still absent until Φ-B4 defines the boundary.
Publication and trust doctrine are explicit, but live execution systems remain gated, review-heavy, and unimplemented.
Nothing yet proves distributed trust, revocation, and authority continuity under runtime-affecting handoff.

must_avoid_replacing: canonical runtime boundary law with release-control convenience
canonical gate law with tool-wrapper behavior
checkpoint authority with stale planning drift

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release, archive, mirror, publication, and trust convenience must not infer canon or permission
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority

final_verdict.reason: The completed Υ-B and Υ-C bands close the specific control-plane maturity gaps that kept Φ-B4 dangerous, while Φ-B5 still remains premature and further Υ work is no longer the required next step before Φ-B4.

notes_on_identifier_policy: Stable family identifiers are preferred over stale older Υ numbering in legacy planning mirrors.
Any later Υ-D labels remain provisional planning names until a checkpoint explicitly freezes them.
